clinical_history: What abnormality is suggested by the ECG in this 71 year old man?
diagnosis: This ECG shows sinus rhythm at a rate of about 70 bpm (there is sinus arrhythmia with a short sinus pause at the beginning). Of particular note is the shortened Q-T interval (about 280 msec) and markedly abbreviated ST segment consistent with hypercalcemia. Subtle doming of the ST-T complex in leads V1-V3 with slight J point/ST elevations in the right chest leads is also sometimes seen in this setting. The patients serum calcium at the time of this ECG was very elevated at 14.8 mg/dL (normal range 8.4-10.2). After extensive evaluation, a diagnosis of primary hyperparathyroidism was made. For a more detailed explanation of this finding see Case # 9.
